title: BBC programme on Elvis Presley's stop at Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: In March 1960, the Rock n' Roll icon set foot on Scottish soil for the first and only time (Image: Prestwick Airport)

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. A NEW programme has shed further light on Elvis Presley's famous stop at Prestwick Airport.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: In March 1960, the Rock n' Roll icon set foot on Scottish soil for the first and only time on his way back from military service in Germany. Despite the stopover lasting just 90 minutes, he was swarmed by adoring fans desperate to get a photo with him.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – New programme on Elvis Presley's famous stop at Prestwick Airport
